This quiet new Auto Dream feature coming to Claude Code looks like a big deal.

It keeps memory clean so Claude doesn't get worse over time. 👀 https://t.co/KIeltmVZ9H
@cursor_ai @ryolu_ @ericzakariasson seems like there's a max zoom level on Glass via cmd-+ - makes the new UI hard to use on larger screens. Would appreciate ability to zoom arbitrarily like old UI 🙏
Why are local LLM wizards always these weird, anonymous accounts https://t.co/KLnVr2Hie2
Claude code using HF papers CLI to do autoresearch https://t.co/dy0DY6VF84
MCP and tools hide complexity. To use agents for everything, they need full access. CLI, rest API, and browser control are most general.

I’ve recently decided to no longer accept requests as a reviewer for scientific papers. Current top AI models do a better job than me for more than 95% of the review process, so with less than 5% effort, it would not be fair for me to take credit & journals don’t like it anyway.

If I look at my personal AI tool usage it’s probably in the $1,000/month range, even with the fancy max plans 

That’s without increased/API token spend.

I could bring it down I’m sure, but time is of the essence.

To be fair to Anthropic/Claude: Can you even imagine how much scaling they have to do?

They’re adding 1 million users/day at the same time as they’re releasing new features daily, and users can absolutely gobble compute.

It’s a miracle these AI services are up at all.

Perplexity now powers Samsung’s Browsing Assist in the Samsung Browser on Galaxy Android and Windows.

Read more: https://t.co/uj3Qs5q4zd
nobody can learn for you, not even your agents

The more I learn to better pair code with Claude, the more I realize the importance of solid foundations and gold-standard references.

As an example, when I ask Claude to write kernels for a model for optimization, it is not entirely starting from scratch. It has access to top

Inference in Unsloth Studio is now ~20% faster.

You can also use older pre-downloaded GGUFs from Hugging Face etc.

AMD chat support for Linux now works. Data Recipes now works on macOS, AMD, CPU setups.

GitHub: https://t.co/aZWYAtakBP
Changelog: https://t.co/npRrgSMYs3 https://t.co/cnZvoG1TeN

Great way to wrap up the week! We’re partnering with Crusoe on a 900MW AI factory in Abilene, Texas. Super excited to add more capacity to our AI fleet. https://t.co/in2Cm43cQU https://t.co/Fv6eR29dHr

Talked with @durov and Telegram folks offered uncomplicated help, welcome @izhukov as new OpenClaw maintainer!

First action point is to figure out why enabling the bot streaming API sometimes causes message dupes. This will make Telegram support so good!
